Claims cite only opaque labels (source_001–003) rather than specific primary authorities — provide statutes, regulations, agency rulings, and controlling state/appellate cases (e.g., IRC/DOL regs, Dynamex/Borello, key circuit decisions).

Claim c001 overgeneralizes: jurisdiction-specific tests differ materially (IRS common-law, DOL economic realities, California ABC/Dynamex). The claim should identify which jurisdiction(s) control and cite the controlling texts/cases.

Claim c002 correctly flags follow-ups but lacks concrete exemplars of conflicting standards and fails to address preemption and overlapping enforcement (federal vs. state agencies, tax vs. labor enforcement). Cite specific conflicting authorities.

Claim c003 lists desirable elements for a decision-grade answer but omits references to the statutory penalty provisions and case law that define remedies and liabilities; also it does not describe how to operationalize the proposed uncertainty/confidence range.
